content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

November 17, 1976


A 76-559

Senator John A. Nejedly
400 Montecillo Drive
Walnut Creek, CA 94595

Dear Senator Nejedly:

     The Chairman has referred your letter of November 4, 1976, to me for a response. There are no restrictions in the Political Reform Act relative to the uses that may be made of surplus campaign funds. Accordingly, nothing in the Political Reform Act would prohibit you from using such funds to retire a loan incurred in a previous campaign. The only obligation under the Political Reform Act would be to correctly report the transaction.

     The Commission's jurisdiction does not extend beyond the Political Reform Act and I, therefore, am not qualified to state whether any other statutory or common law restrictions exist that would affect your proposed transaction. I am informed that the Attorney General is presently is preparing an opinion which relates to the issue of limitations on uses of campaign funds, and I would suggest that you contact that office for advice about statutory and case law restrictions other than the Political Reform Act.

     If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Kenneth Finney
                                        Chief
                                        Legal Division

KF:jo

FPPC
[illegible] 9 9 16 AH '15

John A. Nejedly
400 Montecillo Drive
Walnut Creek, Ca. 94595

November 4, 1976

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

Will you be kind enough to advise if a surplus
in current campaign funding can be utilized to
retire a loan I incurred to finance a previous
campaign.

Very truly yours,

John A. Nejedly

elect Cods 12/400
84507


November 23, 1976

A 90-560

Honorable George Zenovich
Senator, 14th District
State Capitol
Sacramento, California 95614

Dear Senator Zenovich:

This letter will confirm advice given to your administrative aide,
Mike Valles, concerning the use of political funds for other than
campaign related activity.

There is nothing in the Political Reform Act which would prohibit
use of your campaign committee's funds for other than a political
purpose. All expenditures made by the committee must, of course,
be reported on the committee's campaign statement. Since you for the
committee is making a gift to you for the purpose of taking a
vacation, you will be required to report the receipt of the gift
from your committee on your Statement of Economic Interests.

I trust this answers your question. Please let me know if I may
be of additional assistance.

Very truly yours,


Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator

RSK:bw

bcc: Jeanne P. [signature]
